body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
irrlicht_observes: yes

## Reasoning

The scenario's two Expected bullets are (1) the session appears in `ready` within 1s of agent launch and (2) the PID is bound within 1s. Both are directly observable for the aider adapter. Aider writes `.aider.chat.history.md` (a per-cwd markdown chat log) on launch; the daemon's FilesUnderCWD watcher emits `transcript_new` immediately and the classifier lands the session in `ready` inside the first scanner tick. The proc-<PID> pre-session row (~50 ms in the recording) covers any gap.

PID binding happens at the same tick via `pid_discovered`. The recording shows process_exited within 250 ms of birth — aider's headless `--print` mode is extremely short-lived — but session_birth and pid_bind both fire within the spec's 1000 ms budget, well before exit.
caveats: Aider's transcript is markdown, not JSONL; assertions about JSONL line counts (used elsewhere) don't apply. Aider also exits very quickly in --print mode (~200ms), so any post-recording assertions about an idle ready window need to anchor before process_exited.
Headless aider may briefly show the working state if the daemon catches the in-flight LLM call, but for the session-start spec only ready and pid_discovered matter.

sources.note: Identity.Name=aider, Source variant FilesUnderCWD with filename .aider.chat.history.md, ExactName{Name: "aider"} process matcher.
